steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both glossy ibises and little blue herons are drawn to areas with standing water for more than 60 days per year. The question asks where neither species would be found, so the correct choice must describe habitat that inverts this shared preference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — single-species preference only: focuses on one species, not a condition that excludes both. ✗
  • Choice B — vague reference to the other species: too imprecise and not supported by the data in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — confuses metric with breeding season length: misapplies the 60-day figure, which refers to standing water, not breeding duration. ✗
  • Choice D — standing water for far fewer than 60 days per year: directly inverts both species' shared requirement, identifying exactly where neither would be attracted. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
